title: trifolium pratense subsp. kotulae
intro: Trifolium pratense subsp. kotulae, also known as red clover, is a perennial legume native to Europe and Western Asia. It is a subspecies of the white clover and is known for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of red clover include reducing menopausal symptoms, alleviating hot flashes, and improving cardiovascular health. Red clover can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often used to support menopausal health and overall well-being.
